instruction: Fix the off-by-one error in the loop boundary.

input.title: Off-By-One Array Traversal
input.error_message: No compiler error, but runtime behavior prints garbage or crashes due to out-of-bounds access.

output.technical: The loop condition `i <= 5` attempts to access index 5, which is outside the bounds of a 5-element array (valid indices are 0 to 4). This results in undefined behavior.
output.beginner: Your loop goes one step too far. Since arrays start counting at 0, the 5th item is at index 4.
output.analogy: It's like walking a dog on a 4-foot leash, but throwing the ball 5 feet away. The dog will hit the end of the leash and choke.
output.solution: Change the loop condition from `<=` to `<`.
output.prevention: When iterating over arrays, always use `< length` instead of `<= length`.

input.error_message: No compiler error, but runtime behavior prints garbage or crashes due to out-of-bounds access.

output.technical: The loop condition `i <= 5` attempts to access index 5, which is outside the bounds of a 5-element array (valid indices are 0 to 4). This results in undefined behavior.
output.beginner: Your loop goes one step too far. Since arrays start counting at 0, the 5th item is at index 4.
output.analogy: It's like walking a dog on a 4-foot leash, but throwing the ball 5 feet away. The dog will hit the end of the leash and choke.
output.solution: Change the loop condition from `<=` to `<`.
output.prevention: When iterating over arrays, always use `< length` instead of `<= length`.
